title: Justice Dept. Creates $1.8 Billion Fund to Compensate ‘Victims of Lawfare’
description: The arrangement was denounced by critics as a slush fund for supporters of President Trump, possibly including Jan. 6 rioters.
author: Glenn Thrush, Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer

title: ICE Agent Charged in Shooting of a Venezuelan Immigrant in Minnesota
description: The agent is the second federal officer to face felony charges in Minnesota stemming from Operation Metro Surge, the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.
author: Ernesto Londoño and Mitch Smith

title: What is Trump’s $1.776 Billion ‘Anti-Weaponization’ Fund?
description: The Trump administration is creating a $1.8 billion fund to compensate people it says were wronged by the federal government, a group that could be largely made up of the president’s allies.
author: Andrew Duehren

title: Hegseth Campaigns for Trump Loyalist in Kentucky House Race
description: The campaign appearance was an extraordinary breach of military decorum, even for Mr. Hegseth, who has stretched the boundaries of partisan politics as defense secretary.
author: Chris Cameron and Eric Schmitt

title: Conspiracy Trial Will Test Trump’s Aggressive Tactics Against Protesters
description: Prosecutors in Spokane, Wash., are trying three activists who they say conspired to impede federal officers. Legal experts call it a stretch.
author: Anna Griffin

title: Demon Attacks. Teleportation. Astrology. The Supernatural Is Back.
description: One of the oldest and most durable features of human experience is re-emerging.
author: Katya Ungerman

title: How a Funding Pause Derailed an Artificial Heart for Babies
description: James Antaki’s efforts to develop a baby’s heart were close to success when his federal funding was cut off. The grants were eventually restored; rebuilding what was lost wasn’t so easy.
author: Simar Bajaj

title: Brookings Institution Report: Over 100,000 Family Separations in Trump Crackdown
description: The Brookings Institution suggests that federal statistics are an undercount because immigrant parents are not being asked about or not disclosing their American children.
author: Miriam Jordan and Jeff Adelson

title: The Vanity and Inanity of Trump’s White House
description: If you’re not strutting, you’re not selling
author: Frank Bruni

title: What A.I. Kant Do
description: Could we English majors have a future after all?
author: Maureen Dowd

title: The Quest for Clean Hydrogen Moves Underground
description: The dream of clean hydrogen has tantalized energy experts for years, but producing it has been tough. Many start-ups think the answer could lie beneath our feet.
author: Brad Plumer and Ian Willms

title: Trump’s Taiwan Gambit is Already a Gift to China
description: President Trump’s open willingness to hold up a $14 billion Taiwan arms package is a win for Beijing. Now China could be weighing how to keep the weapons on ice for as long as it can.
author: Chris Buckley and Amy Chang Chien

title: Trump Has Done More Than Misjudge Iran
description: The war has revealed the true puniness of Trump’s power
author: Lydia Polgreen

title: Catastrophe Is Emerging in the World’s Most Vulnerable Places
description: The humanitarian relief system, decimated by cuts, faces a grave challenge as the Middle East war causes soaring costs for food, fuel and fertilizer.
author: Peter S. Goodman and Finbarr O’Reilly

title: A Crack in the Polling Floor Puts Trump in New Territory
description: A 37 percent approval rating in a new Times/Siena poll suggests the G.O.P. is facing a big midterm problem despite recent redistricting gains.
author: Nate Cohn

title: Trump’s Approval Sinks Amid Unpopular War, Darkening G.O.P. Prospects
description: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.
author: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker

title: The Supreme Court’s Voting Rights Decision Spurred Swift Action in Louisiana and Alabama
description: The timing brought the court into the middle of a fight to redraw voting maps across the South, even as some primaries were underway.
author: Abbie VanSickle

title: In Closed-Door Talks, U.S. Demands a Major Role in Greenland
description: Greenlandic officials worry about the direction of the negotiations aimed at defusing President Trump’s threats to seize their island. But they have little leverage.
author: Jeffrey Gettleman, Maya Tekeli, Anton Troianovski and Eric Schmitt

title: Actually, Democracy Dies in H.R.
description: New research sheds light on how mediocre employees help would-be authoritarians maintain power.
author: Amanda Taub

title: We Need More ‘Rabid Partisans’ Like Shane Massey
description: Defeating a sclerotic rump of a party is no achievement. Instead, one-party rule enables corruption. It fosters stagnation.
author: David French
